skill.name: react-ui-patterns
skill.description: Guidance for building React UI components with clear loading, error, empty, button, and form submission states.
skill.summary: Apply practical React patterns for async data, visible errors, empty states, and responsive button behavior.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command execution, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns, but review found these are false positives in Markdown examples and ordinary UI text. The skill contains guidance and React snippets only, with no executable scripts, network calls, credential access, or prompt injection text found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fences Flagged as Command Execution
low_findings.description: The external command findings point to Markdown fenced TypeScript and TSX examples, not executable shell or Ruby code. No evidence found of shell execution behavior in the skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are Markdown code fences and React examples. They do not define scripts, invoke shell commands, or instruct the assistant to execute external commands.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern in Descriptive Text
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings correspond to frontmatter and JSX property text using the word description. No hashing, encryption, or cryptographic API usage appears at these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines contain natural language metadata and UI copy fields. There is no evidence of MD5, SHA-1, DES, or another weak cryptographic operation.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Reconnaissance Pattern in UI State Examples
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance findings point to form validation and button disabled state examples. No system inspection, host enumeration, or environment discovery behavior is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines only show React button disabling logic based on validation or loading state. They do not access system commands, files, environment data, or host metadata.

content.value_statement: React interfaces often fail when loading, error, and empty states are handled inconsistently. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code clear patterns for dependable async UI behavior.
actual_capabilities: Guides when to show loading indicators versus cached content.
Defines a hierarchy for inline, toast, banner, and full-screen errors.
Provides React component patterns for error states, empty states, and buttons.
Shows form submission patterns with validation, mutation handling, and user feedback.
Lists practical anti-patterns for spinners, swallowed errors, and duplicate submissions.
limitations: Does not provide a complete component library or installable package.
Does not include automated tests or runnable example projects.
Assumes React-style component patterns and may need adaptation for other frameworks.
Does not cover accessibility requirements in depth.

use_cases.title: Build Async Product Screens
use_cases.description: Create list, detail, and form screens that handle loading, empty data, and failures without confusing users.

use_cases.title: Review UI State Coverage
use_cases.description: Check a React component for missing error messages, duplicate submission risks, and unclear empty states.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team Patterns
use_cases.description: Turn common loading, error, and button behavior into consistent guidance for a React team.

prompt_templates.title: Add Basic UI States
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the react-ui-patterns skill to add loading, error, empty, and success states to this React component.

prompt_templates.title: Fix Loading Behavior
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply the loading state decision tree from react-ui-patterns. Keep cached data visible during refetches and show a loader only when no data exists.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Form Submission
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this form with react-ui-patterns. Ensure validation, disabled submit controls, loading indicators, success feedback, and surfaced errors are handled.

prompt_templates.title: Design a State Pattern
prompt_templates.prompt: Use react-ui-patterns to propose reusable UI state components for lists, forms, and mutations in this React application.

output_examples.input: Improve a product list that shows a spinner on every refetch.
output_examples.output: The response explains that cached products should remain visible during refetches, with a small refresh indicator instead of replacing the list.

output_examples.input: Review a create-item form that logs errors but shows no message.
output_examples.output: The response recommends visible error feedback, disabled submit behavior during submission, and a clear retry or correction path.

output_examples.input: Create empty state guidance for search and first-use screens.
output_examples.output: The response separates no-results messaging from first-use messaging and suggests contextual actions for each case.

best_practices: Show loading indicators only when no useful data can be displayed.
Surface every user-facing failure with clear feedback and a recovery path.
Disable action triggers during async operations to prevent duplicate submissions.
anti_patterns: Replacing cached content with a spinner during every background refetch.
Logging errors without showing users that an operation failed.
Allowing buttons to submit multiple times while a request is pending.

faq.question: Does this skill generate React components?
faq.answer: It provides patterns and examples that an AI assistant can apply when editing or creating React components.

faq.question: Can it help with GraphQL mutations?
faq.answer: Yes. The examples include mutation completion, error handling, and loading state patterns that fit GraphQL workflows.

faq.question: Does it include styling guidance?
faq.answer: Only at a behavioral level. It focuses on UI states, feedback, and component flow rather than visual design systems.

faq.question: Is this skill framework-specific?
faq.answer: It is written for React and React-style components. Some concepts can transfer to other UI frameworks.

faq.question: Does it run commands or install packages?
faq.answer: No. The skill is a Markdown guidance file and does not include executable scripts.

faq.question: When should I use it?
faq.answer: Use it when building or reviewing React components that depend on async data, mutations, or user feedback.
